title: 10 best herbal teas for white tongue
intro_desc: Herbal teas for white tongue are natural remedies that have been used for centuries to soothe and treat the condition. White tongue is characterized by a thick, cheesy coating on the tongue that can cause bad breath, difficulty speaking, and discomfort while eating. Herbal teas like slippery elm, peppermint, and chamomile help to reduce inflammation and remove bacteria from the mouth, promoting a healthy balance of flora. Drinking these teas regularly can improve oral hygiene, alleviate symptoms, and even boost overall well-being, allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods and social activities with confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with white tongue because its soothing properties gently clean and remove bacteria, fungus, and dead cells from the surface of the tongue. The herbal tea's mucilages create a protective barrier that shields the tongue from irritants, reducing inflammation and discomfort. As the tea works to restore balance to the oral environment, it also helps to reduce bad breath and freshens the mouth, promoting overall oral health and hygiene.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root tea helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, which can ease symptoms of white tongue such as discomfort and irritation.

Mucilages and demulcent properties: The thick, gel-like substance in marshmallow root helps to protect and coat the tongue and surrounding tissues, providing a barrier against irritants and soothing dryness and inflammation.

Antimicrobial and antibacterial properties: Marshmallow root tea has been shown to have antimicrobial and antibacterial properties, which can help combat infection and promote healing of the tongue and mouth, reducing symptoms of white tongue such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help to soothe and coat the throat, reducing inflammation associated with white tongue.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, the barks aid in shrinking inflamed tissue and reducing redness on the tongue.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried marshmallow root powder one teaspoon and boiling water two cups.
Measure the dried herb add it to a heat-resistant cup or teapot and pour in.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup discarding solids if any.
Enjoy the tea hot with honey or lemon to taste and drink within six hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with white tongue because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and inflammation on the tongue. The antioxidants present in marigold tea also help to soothe and calm irritated tongues, promoting a healthy and normal color. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of pot marigold tea can help to eliminate any bacteria or fungi that may be contributing to white tongue. Overall, drinking pot marigold tea regularly can lead to a reduction in white tongue symptoms and promotion of a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness associated with a white tongue, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to a white tongue, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Marigold tea's wound-healing compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins, aid in the repair and regeneration of damaged mucous membrane tissue, helping to restore a normal tongue color and texture.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with white tongue.
Seeds: The seeds contain antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that help combat bacterial infections and reduce the severity of white tongue symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the calendula mixture for ten to fifteen minutes in a glass teapot or infuser.
Strain the tea into a ceramic mug using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool for three to five minutes before consumption for optimal potency.
Drink the warm tea three times a day, ideally thirty minutes after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with white tongue because of its potent antimicrobial properties that help combat Candida overgrowth, a common cause of white tongue. The tea's antifungal compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, work to suppress fungal growth and promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria. Additionally, purple coneflower teas' anti-inflammatory properties soothe oral mucosa and reduce inflammation, allowing the tongue to return to its normal pink color and texture. By promoting a balanced oral ecosystem, these teas can help resolve white tongue issues naturally.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple Coneflower tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth and throat, which may contribute to a white tongue.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial compounds, including triterpenoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may cause infections or irritation leading to a white tongue.

Antioxidant properties: Purple Coneflower tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to oral health issues like white tongue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe the mouth and tongue.
Seeds: Contain antioxidant-rich oils that promote oral health and fight against bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea roots and flowers for 7-10 days using a dehydrator or air drying method.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea root and 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers per 8 oz of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients with boiling water in a large mug or teapot and let steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then sweeten with honey if desired before drinking immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with white tongue because of its natural detoxifying properties. The tea's bitter flavor stimulates saliva production, which helps to remove bacteria and other debris that can cause discoloration on the tongue. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help to flush out toxins and excess mucus from the body, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment. By drinking dandelion tea regularly, individuals may experience an improvement in their tongue health, leading to a reduction or elimination of white tongue symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to a white tongue by soothing irritated mucous membranes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea help protect the tongue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall oral health and potentially reducing white patches.

Astringent properties: Dandelion tea's astringent compounds can help dry up excess mucus and reduce swelling in the mouth, which can help eliminate the cause of a white tongue (e.g., thrush or leukoplakia) by creating an environment less conducive to fungal growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the mouth.
Fruits: The fruits of the dandelion plant contain bitter compounds that help stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of white tongue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dandelion roots and leaves from a trusted source or purchase them from a reputable store.
Dry the harvested plants in a warm dark place for several hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat settings.
Combine one teaspoon of dried dandelion root and two teaspoons of dried dandelion leaves with one cup of boiling water to make tea.
Steep the herbal mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid before straining the tea.
Drink the warm tea three times a day after meals or as needed to help reduce white tongue symptoms and promote overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with white tongue because they possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that effectively combat oral infections and inflammation. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Elder tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi on the tongue, which can contribute to the development of a white or yellowish coating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tea may help to reduce swelling and redness in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate discomfort associated with a white tongue.
Antioxidant properties: Elder tea's high antioxidant content may help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the development of a white or coated tongue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, elder roots help soothe and calm white tongue inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing a combination of alkaloids and glycosides, elder rhyzomes are effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with white tongue.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Bring filtered water to a boil and pour it over the sambucus nigra flowers in the cup or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container or teacup.
Serve the sambucus nigra tea warm or at room temperature, drinking a minimum of 1 cup twice daily for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with white tongue because it has natural antibacterial properties that help to eliminate bad breath-causing bacteria, reducing the growth of Candida albicans that can cause a thick white or yellowish coating on the tongue. The menthol in peppermint also soothes and calms the tongue, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to its appearance. By drinking herbal peppermint tea regularly, individuals can promote a healthy oral environment, banishing the appearance of a white tongue for good.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tea's antimicrobial compounds help kill bacteria, viruses, and fungi on the surface of the tongue, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint tea reduce swelling and irritation on the tongue, which can cause white patches to form due to inflammation and trauma.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage tongue tissue and contribute to the formation of white patches, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the mouth and tongue.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, contains compounds that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat infections that may contribute to white tongue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves in the morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Rinse the mint leaves thoroughly with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Measure out 15-20 dried peppermint leaves or 30-40 grams of fresh mint leaves.
Steep the mint leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy hot or add honey and lemon to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with white tongue because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat Candida, a fungus that can cause white patches on the tongue. The soothing and calming effects of licorice tea also reduce inflammation and irritation, promoting a healthy tongue environment. Additionally, licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, further aiding in the resolution of white tongue symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Licorice tea helps to combat bacteria, fungi, and viruses that can contribute to a white tongue coating by promoting a healthy balance of gut flora.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, helping to soothe an irritated white tongue.

Soilting and protective properties: The saponins present in licorice tea help to coat and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, creating a barrier against irritants and further reducing the appearance of a white tongue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming white tongue.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat infections that may contribute to white tongue.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in two cups of water for ten minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional five minutes before straining the liquid.
Combine the filtered tea with one tablespoon of honey and stir until dissolved.
Allow the tea to cool at room temperature for fifteen to twenty minutes before serving.
Drink the tea three times a day for a minimum of two weeks to observe improvements in tongue health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with white tongue because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the mouth and throat, allowing the normal color of the tongue to show through. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antifungal compounds help eliminate harmful bacteria and fungi that can cause an overgrowth of yeast on the tongue, leading to a white or off-white appearance. By drinking ginger tea regularly, you can promote a healthy balance in your mouth and restore a normal tongue color.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ginger tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to white tongue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate symptoms of white tongue like discomfort and soreness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of ginger tea can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, promoting overall oral health and potentially reducing the appearance of a white tongue.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat associated with white tongue.
Roots: High in antioxidant properties, roots combat free radicals that can contribute to white tongue symptoms and promote overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginger roots with thick, smooth skin for maximum bioavailability.
Peel the ginger roots using a vegetable peeler and slice them into thin pieces for efficient extraction.
Boil one teaspoon of sliced ginger in one cup of water for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and discard excess ginger particles.
Drink the warm ginger tea within 15-30 minutes after preparation, ideally twice daily, for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with white tongue because of its natural antibacterial properties that effectively combat the growth of Candida albicans, a common fungal infection that causes white patches on the tongue. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe and calm the inflamed areas, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, lemongrass' detoxifying properties help to eliminate toxins and bacteria from the mouth, promoting a healthy balance of flora and fauna in the oral cavity, ultimately leading to a smooth and pink tongue surface.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to a white tongue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce swelling and irritation on the tongue, which may contribute to its whitening appearance.
Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass tea's high antioxidant content can help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and lead to discoloration of the tongue, such as a white coating.

Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain compounds that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation associated with white tongue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning for maximum potency and quality.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the solids after 10 minutes of preparation time.
Drink the warm tea immediately, taking 2-3 cups throughout the day for optimal relief from white tongue symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with white tongue because it possesses antibacterial properties that effectively combat the bacteria that cause the buildup of debris on the tongue. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for a healthier oral environment. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's natural antimicrobial agents work to break down and eliminate the thick layers of dead cells, restoring the tongue's natural pinkish color and promoting fresher breath.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: These properties help to reduce the presence of bacteria and inflammation on the tongue, which can contribute to a white coating or "coated tongue" appearance.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea help to dry up excess mucus and reduce the thickness of the white coating on the tongue, promoting a healthier oral environment.
Antifungal properties: Some studies have shown that Tasmanian Blue Gum has antifungal properties, which can help to combat fungal infections that may be contributing to the white tongue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the inflammation in the tongue.
Barks: The barks of the tree contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat the underlying infections causing white tongue, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried eucalyptus leaves from eucalyptus globulus trees in the ratio of one teaspoon for every 8 ounces of water.
Boil filtered or bottled water in a teapot and let it cool down to a temperature of around 200-205 degrees fahrenheit.
Add the measured dose of dried eucalyptus leaves to the cooled-down boiling water and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids after 10-15 seconds.
Drink the herbal tea immediately, making sure to consume at least one cup twice daily with meals or as directed by your healthcare provider.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat white tongue with herbal teas is to brew a strong infusion of chamomile, calendula, or sage. These herbs have antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat infection. Strain the tea and swish it in your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Repeat this process two to three times a day to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to white tongue that are treated with herbal teas are various oral health issues. For instance, thrush, which causes a white or yellowish patch on the tongue and inner cheeks, can be treated with tea made from herbs like slippery elm, licorice root, and calendula. Herbal teas containing sage, chamomile, and peppermint have also been used to soothe mouth sores, gum inflammation, and bad breath.
